page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with single-thread-per-core execution and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on external storage options. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average tier, supported by average single-threaded gzip compression and text processing speeds. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, register in the weak to poor categories. Memory bandwidth tests also show lower throughput, with uncached read and write speeds landing in the bottom quartile.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high memory capacity relative to its processing core count, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute scaling. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, caching layers, text processing, and general-purpose applications that benefit from a 32.0 GB memory footprint but are constrained by low parallel processing demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance in text processing and compression, contrasted by weak multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective memory allocation of 8.0 GB per core for workloads that prioritize memory capacity over raw compute power. It is best suited for caching systems, lightweight databases, and text processing applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute requirements.
